content: Yes, according to the Krantz-Morrison framework, when the ROIC minus WACC spread falls below 200 basis points (2%), it recommends switching from a perpetuity growth model to a mean-reversion decay model for terminal value estimation. Since your current spread is 180 basis points (1.8%), which is below the 200 bps threshold, you should make the switch for this year's DCF terminal value calculation. This approach better reflects the expectation that excess returns will mean-revert rather than persist indefinitely when the spread is low.
